_meta.title: gcp_indicator_library
_meta.description: Library of customer-facing text for every indicator at every band of the GCP chain. Layered annotation ON TOP OF the canonical chain definition (gcp_confidence_score_v2_1_LOCKED.json). References chain indicator IDs (L3I_GCP_NNN). PER-POINT CHAIN: text fields use {point_id} placeholder which the rendering layer substitutes with actual GCP point identifier (e.g., GCP-003) at render time.

three_tier_architecture.tier_2_library_this_file: gcp_indicator_library_v2_1.json — customer-voice text per indicator per band

_meta.text_authorship_status: CLAUDE-DRAFTED PLACEHOLDER. Per pattern Q1 confirmation: structure locked, library text marked as placeholder pending domain refinement.
_meta.per_point_chain_notes: GCP is a per-point chain. Each survey may have multiple GCPs. Indicator scores are computed per-point; chain score aggregates across points. Per-point findings surface in customer view with point-ID attribution per Q-GCP-3 lock. Text placeholders use {point_id} which renders as actual point identifier.
_meta.device_type_conditionality: L3I_GCP_005 (antenna_height) auto-scores 100 for CB_X / AEROPOINT devices (factory-known height). L3I_GCP_002 (integrity) score logic differs by device class (DGPS expects operation log; CB_X/AEROPOINT do not).

schema_notes.point_id_placeholder: Use {point_id} in verified_statement, impact, and actions text. Rendering layer substitutes with actual point ID (e.g., GCP-003).
schema_notes.gate_scope: Per-indicator field: chain_level_when_all_points / per_point_only / none. Distinguishes chain-level vs per-point gating behavior.

L3I_GCP_001.name: Coverage
L3I_GCP_001.verified_statement: GCP {point_id} was occupied for the full required duration with adequate buffer time.

bands.label: Coverage 100% + pre-occupation buffer ≥120s + post-occupation buffer ≥60s
Coverage 100%, pre-occupation buffer 60-120s
Coverage 100% but pre-occupation buffer <60s
bands.impact: GCP {point_id} convergence may be incomplete at occupation start. Early epochs can carry residual error.
actions: Allow 2 min for the device to converge before walking away from the point next time
Review processed sigma on early epochs at this point
If accuracy is critical, consider trimming noisy early data

bands.label: GCP {point_id} has no usable occupation data (per-point coverage failed)
bands.impact: GCP {point_id} cannot contribute to control. If multiple points fail, chain-level hard gate fires.
actions: Re-occupy this specific point
Allow full occupation duration with proper buffer
Verify recording started before occupation and stopped after

L3I_GCP_001.derivation: Per-point coverage gate (score=0) degrades that point's contribution but does NOT fire chain-level hard gate. Chain-level hard gate fires only when EVERY designated GCP has score=0 (Q-GCP-1 lock). Threshold 88 reflects industry PPK convergence guidance for static occupations.

L3I_GCP_002.name: Integrity
L3I_GCP_002.verified_statement: GCP {point_id} occupation completed normally with no interruptions and battery state healthy.

bands.label: Clean session, no shutdowns, battery ≥20%
Operation log absent — occupation integrity unconfirmed
bands.impact: Cannot verify GCP {point_id} occupation completed cleanly. Data may still be usable but audit trail is incomplete.
actions: Verify recorded file size matches expected occupation duration
Inspect last epoch timestamp against planned end
Ensure operation log uploads with device files next time

bands.label: Device shutdown during occupation at point {point_id}
bands.impact: Device shut down unexpectedly during occupation at GCP {point_id}. Recording may be truncated or corrupted.
actions: Inspect device file around shutdown timestamp
If shutdown occurred outside occupation window, data may still process
If shutdown occurred during occupation, re-occupy this point

L3I_GCP_002.derivation: Score 60 (operation log absent) is conservative middle band per base_station v2.1 pattern. Conditional on device_type: CB_X / AEROPOINT devices don't expect operation logs (factory-known operation), so score logic differs by device class.

L3I_GCP_003.name: Format
L3I_GCP_003.verified_statement: GCP {point_id} data is in supported format with complete header and dual-frequency observations.

bands.label: Supported version, complete header, dual-frequency
Single-frequency or incomplete header at point {point_id}
bands.impact: Single-frequency observations at GCP {point_id} mean ionospheric error cannot be modeled. Accuracy degrades during active solar weather.
actions: Verify device was configured for dual-frequency at this point
Patch missing header fields if known
Expect reduced accuracy at this point under high Kp index

bands.label: File version not supported by PPK software (point {point_id})
bands.impact: Device file from GCP {point_id} cannot be ingested directly. Data is fine — format conversion required.
actions: Convert file to a supported version using vendor or standard converter
Verify converted file passes format validation
Resubmit for processing

L3I_GCP_003.derivation: Following base_station v2.1 Q2 lock pattern: version-unsupported is review (material), not resurvey. Data is fine; format conversion is fixable without recollection.

L3I_GCP_004.name: Continuity
L3I_GCP_004.verified_statement: Continuous observations at GCP {point_id} with minimal cycle slips.

bands.label: No gaps or minor gaps only (<60s), minimal cycle slips
Gap >60s detected at point {point_id} — PPK must re-converge
bands.impact: GCP {point_id} lost satellite tracking for >60s. PPK must re-converge after the gap, reducing accuracy in that window.
actions: Check whether occupation was interrupted (operator walked away?)
Investigate cause — signal blockage, brief power loss, person standing on point?
Position next occupation in clearer location

bands.label: Multiple disturbances or extensive gaps at point {point_id}
bands.impact: Significant disturbance pattern at GCP {point_id}. Position oscillations likely in processed output.
actions: Inspect raw file for repeated signal loss events
Consider re-occupying this point in a less disturbed environment
Flag for review during processing residual analysis

L3I_GCP_004.derivation: 60s gap threshold from industry PPK best practice. Feeds disturbance composite.

L3I_GCP_005.name: Antenna height
L3I_GCP_005.verified_statement: Antenna height at GCP {point_id} documented to ARP, matching device file delta-H.

bands.label: Factory-known antenna height (CB_X / AEROPOINT — auto-100)
DGPS device — vertical measurement to ARP, ≥3 measurements, matches RINEX
Slant measurement, single measurement, or RINEX delta-H disagreement at point {point_id}
bands.impact: Antenna height measurement at GCP {point_id} carries higher uncertainty. Wrong height shifts elevation reference for that point.
actions: Verify slant-to-vertical conversion was applied at point {point_id}
Reconcile entered value against RINEX delta-H for this point
Re-measure vertical-to-ARP if uncertain

bands.label: Antenna height missing at GCP {point_id} (PER-POINT GATE)
bands.impact: GCP {point_id} elevation reference is unknown. This point cannot contribute to elevation control. NOT a chain-level hard gate — other points still score.
actions: Enter antenna height for point {point_id} now if recoverable from field notes
If not recoverable, re-occupy this specific point with measured height
Measure 3 times vertical-to-ARP, average for best confidence

L3I_GCP_005.derivation: PER-POINT GATE only (Q-GCP-1 lock). Missing antenna height on one GCP degrades that point's contribution but does NOT fire chain-level hard gate. CB_X/AEROPOINT devices auto-score 100 (factory-known). Device-type conditional logic per chain meta.

L3I_GCP_006.name: Device ID match
L3I_GCP_006.verified_statement: Device ID at GCP {point_id} matches between operator form and RINEX header.

bands.label: Form device ID matches RINEX header
Device ID unconfirmed at point {point_id} (one side missing)
bands.impact: Cannot verify which physical device captured GCP {point_id} data. Audit trail incomplete.
actions: Check field notes for device serial used at this point
Update either form or header with correct device ID
Document for QA

bands.label: Device ID mismatch at point {point_id} (form vs file)
bands.impact: Form-declared device differs from device that wrote the file at GCP {point_id}. Wrong device profile may produce systematic positioning bias.
actions: Verify physical device used at this point — check field photos if available
Update whichever source is incorrect
Re-process with correct device profile

L3I_GCP_006.derivation: Reviewer-blocking flag — significant audit trail concern. Mismatch indicates form was filled incorrectly or wrong file was uploaded for this point.

L3I_GCP_007.name: Antenna type match
L3I_GCP_007.verified_statement: Antenna type at GCP {point_id} matches between form and RINEX header.

bands.label: Form antenna matches RINEX header
Antenna type mismatch at point {point_id}
bands.impact: Wrong antenna profile at GCP {point_id} means wrong ANTEX calibration — systematic position bias from millimeters to centimeters at this point.
actions: Verify physical antenna at point {point_id} against form selection
Update whichever source is incorrect
Re-process this point with correct ANTEX profile

L3I_GCP_007.derivation: Type-string consistency check only — not a true ANTEX phase-center calibration (Stage-2 concern in pre_processing chain).

L3I_GCP_008.name: Multipath
L3I_GCP_008.verified_statement: Clean signal environment at GCP {point_id} — low C/N0 variance.

bands.label: Low C/N0 variance (<2.5 dB-Hz) — clean signal at point {point_id}
Moderate multipath risk at point {point_id}
bands.impact: GCP {point_id} was near reflective surfaces. PPK can usually handle it but residuals may show position oscillations at this point.
actions: Check processed residuals for oscillations at point {point_id}
Position next occupation ≥10m from buildings/vehicles/water
Choose open-sky locations for high-stakes work

bands.label: High multipath risk at point {point_id} (>4.0 dB-Hz variance)
bands.impact: GCP {point_id} was clearly in a reflective environment. PPK output for this point likely has elevated residuals.
actions: Inspect processed position output for this point
Consider re-occupying at cleaner site if accuracy is critical
Scout point locations for reflective surfaces in future surveys

L3I_GCP_008.derivation: C/N0 variance as multipath proxy is a standard signal-quality metric. Threshold values are heuristic — calibration against measured PPK residuals is a future deliverable per chain calibration roadmap.

L3I_GCP_009.name: Acquisition
L3I_GCP_009.verified_statement: Device at GCP {point_id} acquired satellites within normal startup time.

bands.label: <2 min — normal startup at point {point_id}
>2 min — slow startup at point {point_id} (hygiene signal)
bands.impact: Device was slow to lock onto satellites at GCP {point_id}. Usually cold start. Recurring slowness can indicate device health issues, but rarely affects this point's data quality.
actions: Check device location for obstructions at this point
Verify device firmware and battery state
Service unit if slow acquisition is recurring across multiple occupations

L3I_GCP_009.derivation: Following base_station Q4 lock pattern: acquisition slowness is hygiene signal, not deliverable-quality concern. Downgraded to minor (audit-only, doesn't drive chain recommendation).

L3I_GCP_010.name: Ionospheric risk
L3I_GCP_010.verified_statement: Either calm geomagnetic conditions or dual-frequency observations at GCP {point_id}.

bands.label: Low Kp (≤4) OR dual-frequency device — ionospheric error modeled
Moderate Kp (5-6) and single-frequency at point {point_id}
bands.impact: Active solar weather + single-frequency device at GCP {point_id} means ionospheric delay cannot be modeled fully.
actions: Check NOAA SWPC Kp index for occupation window
Prefer dual-frequency devices for future surveys
Document for QA — expected accuracy reduced at this point

bands.label: Severe geomagnetic storm (Kp ≥7) and single-frequency at point {point_id}
bands.impact: Severe geomagnetic conditions at GCP {point_id} during occupation. Position errors can reach decimeters under these conditions with single-frequency receivers.
actions: Re-occupy this point during calmer space weather if accuracy is critical
Replace single-frequency devices with dual-frequency for future surveys
Reschedule surveys when forecast Kp ≥5 and accuracy is critical

L3I_GCP_010.derivation: Kp ≥5 threshold from NOAA space weather scale (G1+ geomagnetic storm). Kp ≥7 represents severe (G3+) storm. External NOAA SWPC API dependency — automation pending per chain calibration roadmap.
